# fail2zig

> fail2zig is a modern intrusion prevention system for Linux — a single static musl binary with zero runtime dependencies that replaces fail2ban. Drop-in compatibility with fail2ban `jail.conf` + `filter.d` config. Kernel-native ban enforcement via direct netlink (nftables preferred; ipset and iptables as fallbacks). Written in Zig, AGPL-3.0-or-later.

## Identity

- Name: fail2zig
- Category: intrusion prevention / log-based ban manager
- Language: Zig 0.14.1
- License: AGPL-3.0-or-later
- Repository: https://github.com/ul0gic/fail2zig
- Website: https://fail2zig.com
- Current release: v0.1.0 (2026-04-24)
- Author: ul0gic (https://github.com/ul0gic)

## One-liner install

```
curl -fsSL https://github.com/ul0gic/fail2zig/raw/main/scripts/install.sh | sudo bash
```

## Core capabilities

- Static musl binary, 877 KB stripped (x86_64-linux-musl); 801 KB (aarch64)
- Zero runtime dependencies (no Python, no libc dynamic linkage, no shell-out)
- Comptime-specialized parser — no runtime regex engine in the process
- Pure netlink for nftables bans (no libnftnl, no shell-out to `nft`)
- Argv-based fallback backends: iptables, ipset
- Bounded memory with hard ceiling (default 64 MB)
- Per-IP 128-slot ring buffer for sliding-window attempt tracking
- Atomic CRC-checked state persistence across restarts
- Built-in HTTP: `/metrics` (Prometheus), `/api/bans` (JSON), `/events` (WebSocket RFC 6455)
- Unix-domain-socket IPC with `SO_PEERCRED` auth for the `fail2zig-client` CLI
- Hardened systemd unit: `CAP_NET_ADMIN` + `CAP_DAC_READ_SEARCH` only

## Filters shipped (15 built-in)

- sshd (9 OpenSSH patterns, 7.x / 8.x / 9.x)
- nginx-http-auth, nginx-limit-req, nginx-botsearch
- apache-auth, apache-badbots, apache-overflows
- postfix, dovecot, courier
- named (BIND)
- vsftpd, proftpd, mysqld
- recidive (meta-filter for repeat offenders)

## Relationship to fail2ban

fail2zig is a drop-in replacement. It reads fail2ban's config format natively via `fail2zig --import-config /etc/fail2ban`, which:

- Parses `jail.conf` + `jail.local` + `jail.d/*.conf` with correct override semantics
- Translates Python regex patterns in `filter.d/` to fail2zig's comptime DSL
- Maps action names in `action.d/` to native backends (`iptables-*` → iptables, `nftables-*` → nftables, `ipset-*` → ipset)
- Emits a validated native TOML config

An existing fail2ban deployment migrates with one command, zero rewrites.

Differences from fail2ban (architectural, not competitive):

- Single static binary vs Python runtime + libraries
- Comptime-specialized parsers vs general-purpose regex engine
- Direct netlink to kernel vs shelling out to `iptables` / `nft` CLI
- Hard memory ceiling vs unbounded GC growth
- Zig vs Python

## Measured performance (v0.1.0, ReleaseSafe, stripped)

- Parse throughput: 5.96M lines/sec
- Ban latency: p50 365 ns, p99 932 ns
- Memory under attack (50K unique IPs): 21,845 entries resident, 15,606 evictions, ceiling never exceeded
- Resident memory under load: 22 MB
- Test suite: 595 passing, 0 leaks, 0 warnings

## Primary docs

- [Home](https://fail2zig.com/): install, quick start, feature overview
- [Why fail2zig](https://fail2zig.com/why): operator-honest argument for replacing fail2ban
- [Threat model](https://fail2zig.com/threat-model): STRIDE analysis, trust boundaries, fail-closed matrix
- [Docs](https://fail2zig.com/docs): reference, guides, architecture deep-dives
- [Changelog](https://fail2zig.com/changelog): release history with commit refs
- [Demo](https://fail2zig.com/demo): live honeypot streaming real attacker bans
- [Privacy](https://fail2zig.com/privacy): data handling (no cookies, no tracking, no telemetry)
- [Terms](https://fail2zig.com/terms): use of the site

## Supported platforms (v0.1.0)

- Linux x86_64 (musl-static)
- Linux aarch64 (musl-static)

Not yet shipped: armv7, mips (tracked as SYS-009), Windows, macOS.

## Security reporting

Vulnerabilities should be reported via GitHub Private Security Advisories, not public issues. 48-hour acknowledgement, coordinated disclosure. See https://github.com/ul0gic/fail2zig/security/advisories/new and the `SECURITY.md` at the repository root.

## Contributing

Contributions welcome. The highest-leverage area is new filter patterns for modern services — fail2ban's filter library stopped expanding around 2015. Candidates: Docker, Kubernetes, Traefik, Caddy, Vaultwarden, Authelia, Keycloak, Gitea, Jellyfin, Immich, LLM endpoints (Ollama, OpenWebUI, LocalAI), PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB, Grafana, Prometheus. See the repository `README.md` "Contributing" section.
